Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 199. (no title) (CT 53 734)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
oo beginning broken away

(Beginning destroyed)

o 1'1'

⸢a-na⸣ [LUGAL EN-ia]

(1) ......] I have s[ent ...] to [the king, my lord].

o 2'2'

ú-⸢se⸣-[bi-la x x]

o 3'3'

ina UGU LÚ.[x x x]

(3) As to the [...] official of Šarru-[...] about whom the king [my lord] wrote [to me]: "Why has he not come?" I am right now sending him to the king, my lord.

o 4'4'

ša mMAN—[x x x x]

o 5'5'

ša LUGAL [be-lí]

Bottom
b.e. 6'b.e. 6'

iš-pur-[an-ni]

Reverse
r 1r 1

ma-a a-[ta-a]

r 22

la il-[li-ka]

r 33

ú-ma ⸢an⸣-[nu-rig]

r 44

ina UGU LUGAL [EN-ia]

r 55

ú-se-⸢bi⸣-[la-šu]

rest uninscribed

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P314144/.
